businessSummary.oneLiner: The world's largest EV maker and a 'Physical AI Platform' spanning autonomous driving, robotics, energy, and AI chips
businessSummary.description: Tesla has expanded beyond EV sales into FSD autonomous driving, Cybercab robotaxis, Optimus humanoid robots, and Megapack energy storage. FY2025 revenue was $94.8B with GAAP EPS of $1.08, and Q4 2025 gross margin hit 20.1%, a two-year high. Key growth catalysts including Cybercab production and Optimus Gen 3 are set to ramp starting April 2026.
howTheyMakeMoney: Energy storage and generation (Megapack, Powerwall, Solar)
Services and charging (Supercharger network, insurance, OTA upgrades)

keyMetrics.name: P/E (TTM)
keyMetrics.interpretation: Extreme premium vs S&P 500 avg (~23x) and auto sector avg (~16x). Reflects AI/robotics/energy optionality but invites overvaluation debate

keyMetrics.name: Forward P/E
keyMetrics.interpretation: Based on FY2026 EPS consensus. Growth expectations are high but require time to materialize

keyMetrics.name: Gross Margin
keyMetrics.interpretation: Q4 2025 hit a two-year high of 20.1%. Margin recovery signal despite pricing pressure

keyMetrics.name: Revenue TTM
keyMetrics.interpretation: FY2025 annual revenue. Slight YoY decline but energy segment driving growth

keyMetrics.name: FCF TTM
keyMetrics.interpretation: Healthy cash generation. $44B total cash provides buffer for massive capex

keyMetrics.name: Debt-to-Equity
keyMetrics.interpretation: Strong balance sheet. Among the lowest leverage in large-cap tech

growthDrivers.title: Samsung AI6 Chip $16.5B Foundry Deal Confirmed
growthDrivers.description: Samsung confirmed at its AGM that it will produce Tesla's AI6 (HW6) chip at its Taylor, Texas fab using its 2nm SF2P process. The deal is valued at $16.5B through 2033. However, Electrek (3/12) reports the AI6 MPW run has been delayed by about 6 months due to Samsung's yield challenges.

growthDrivers.title: Terafab Launch March 21 (D-2): World's Largest 2nm AI Chip Fab
growthDrivers.description: Terafab will vertically integrate logic processing, memory storage, and advanced packaging in a single facility. $20B+ investment. The March 21 launch is a ceremonial groundbreaking, with AI5 chip small-batch production expected in 2026 and volume production in 2027.

growthDrivers.title: LG Energy Solution $4.3B LFP Battery Plant Confirmed
growthDrivers.description: The U.S. government confirmed the Lansing, Michigan LFP cell manufacturing facility contract. Megapack 3 cells to begin production in 2027, achieving 5 MWh per unit (28% increase from 3.9 MWh).

growthDrivers.title: Cybercab April Production Start, Macrohard AI Platform
growthDrivers.description: Cybercab production line designed for hundreds per week. Macrohard (Tesla-xAI joint venture) enters enterprise AI, diversifying beyond vehicles.

growthDrivers.title: China Sales +35% YoY in Jan-Feb, Global Recovery Signal
growthDrivers.description: Tesla China-made EV sales reached 127,728 units in Jan-Feb, up 35%+ YoY. BYD numbers actually declined, signaling a market share reversal.

competitiveAdvantage.summary: 50%+ U.S. EV market share leader. 8.2B miles of FSD data, Supercharger NACS standard, and vertical integration form the core moat. Samsung AI6 foundry deal accelerates custom chip ecosystem.

moats.description: 8.2 billion FSD miles driven. Overwhelmingly more training data than any competitor.
In-house batteries (4680), custom chips (Terafab + Samsung AI6), software (FSD), charging infrastructure (Supercharger).
NACS is the North American EV charging standard. GM, Ford, and Rivian depend on Tesla Superchargers.

competitors.name: BYD
competitors.detail: Global EV sales leader (2.26M in 2025 vs Tesla's 1.64M). 11 new models, 640-mile sedan, 5-min charging tech

recentNews.headline: Samsung confirms Tesla AI6 chip 2nm production at Taylor, Texas fab ($16.5B deal)
recentNews.significance: Samsung AGM officially confirmed AI6 (HW6) foundry contract. Volume production target H2 2027. Tesla's dual-foundry strategy (Samsung + TSMC) takes shape

recentNews.headline: Musk hints at new Tesla Roadster unveiling: 'next-level banger'
recentNews.significance: Roadster prototype design changes confirmed. No exact date but building anticipation

recentNews.headline: U.S. government confirms Tesla-LG Energy Solution $4.3B LFP battery deal
recentNews.significance: Megapack 3 dedicated battery cell plant. Major energy supply chain security

recentNews.headline: China Jan-Feb sales 127,728 units, +35% YoY (BYD declined)
recentNews.significance: China market reversal signal. Global delivery recovery expectations strengthened

recentNews.headline: Tesla AI6 chip delayed ~6 months as Samsung 2nm production slips
recentNews.significance: AI6 MPW production delayed. Dual-foundry strategy provides insurance but Samsung yield maturity is key

analystOpinions.notableComment: Barchart: 43 analysts, Hold consensus. 15 Strong Buys vs 9 Strong Sells, deeply divided. Mean target $408.42 offers only 3% upside.

risks.title: BYD Competition Accelerating, Global EV Share Reversal
risks.description: BYD sold 2.26M EVs in 2025 (+28%) vs Tesla's 1.64M (-8%). 11 new models, 640-mile sedan, 5-minute charging. Aggressive expansion in emerging markets.

risks.title: Terafab + Samsung AI6 Execution Risk
risks.description: Tesla has zero semiconductor manufacturing experience yet plans the world's largest 2nm fab. Samsung AI6 is already 6 months delayed with unresolved yield issues. $20B+ capex at risk.

risks.title: Extreme Valuation, P/E 395x
risks.description: TTM P/E 395x, Forward P/E 193x. Any growth slowdown or competitive intensification could trigger multiple compression.

risks.title: NHTSA FSD Investigation, Cybercab Regulatory Hurdles
risks.description: 2.88M vehicle FSD investigation, Austin robotaxi traffic violations, railroad crossing incident. Cybercab requires regulatory approval before U.S. sales.

risks.title: Capital Raise Risk, Potential Shareholder Dilution
risks.description: 2026 capex guidance $20B+. Electrek suggests first capital raise since 2020. Dilution risk.

risks.title: Musk Leadership Spread + DOGE Controversy
risks.description: Simultaneously running Tesla, xAI, SpaceX, X, DOGE, and Macrohard. Focus dilution concerns persist.

buyReasons.title: Samsung AI6 $16.5B + LG $4.3B: Chip + Battery Supply Chain Secured
buyReasons.rationale: Both AI chip foundry and Megapack battery cell supply confirmed simultaneously. Key bottlenecks for AI chip production and energy expansion being resolved.

buyReasons.title: Terafab March 21 Launch + Cybercab April Production
buyReasons.rationale: Terafab groundbreaking in 2 days, Cybercab production next month. Near-term catalysts are lined up.

buyReasons.title: China Sales +35%, Energy Revenue at 5-Quarter High
buyReasons.rationale: China recovery signals global delivery rebound. Energy segment gross profit reached $1.1B, reducing EV dependency.

buyReasons.title: Optimus Robot + Fremont Factory Conversion
buyReasons.rationale: Model S/X discontinued to convert Fremont for Optimus production. If successful, TAM expands to $12T+ robotics market.

overallOpinion: Samsung confirmed Tesla AI6 chip 2nm production at its AGM. The $16.5B deal will see chips made at the Taylor, Texas fab from H2 2027. However, the AI6 prototype (MPW) is already 6 months behind schedule, and Samsung's yield issues remain unresolved.

Terafab breaks ground in 2 days (March 21), and the LG $4.3B battery deal is already locked in. Tesla's vertical integration strategy is gaining substance with both chip and battery supply chains materializing simultaneously.

Yet the stock sits flat at $398, which tells you the market has priced in a lot. At 395x P/E with 43 analysts at a Hold consensus, there's only 3% upside to the $408 target. It's holding above the 200-day MA ($394) but can't break through the 50-day ($415).

investmentVerdict.summary: Samsung AI6 and LG battery deals secure the supply chain, with Terafab groundbreaking just days away. But the AI6 delay, 395x P/E, and analyst Hold consensus suggest patience rather than aggressive buying at current levels.

sources.name: SamMobile (Samsung AI6)
sources.description: Samsung confirms AI6 chip production at Taylor, Texas fab on 2nm process, $16.5B deal

sources.description: Tesla AI6 chip MPW delayed ~6 months due to Samsung 2nm yield issues

sources.name: Electrek (LG Battery Deal)

sources.name: Tesla Oracle (Roadster)

x.hook: Tesla AI6 chip: Samsung confirms $16.5B deal, Terafab D-2
x.text:
$TSLA (Mar 19) $398.09. Samsung confirmed AI6 chip 2nm production at its AGM. $16.5B deal at Taylor, Texas. But prototypes are 6 months late. Terafab groundbreaking in 2 days.

1/4
thread:
Samsung locked in AI6 (HW6) foundry, but Electrek says MPW production slipped 6 months. Yield issues remain. The dual-foundry strategy (Samsung + TSMC) provides insurance.

2/4

LG $4.3B battery deal already confirmed. Chip + battery supply chains materializing simultaneously. China sales +35%. Catalysts are stacking up.

3/4

The catch: 395x P/E, 43 analysts at Hold, target $408 (+3%). Holding above 200-day MA ($394) but stuck below 50-day ($415). Plenty of catalysts, no rush. Hold.

4/4

threads.hook: Samsung confirms Tesla AI6 chip production. But it's 6 months late.
threads.text:
$TSLA (Mar 19) $398.09

Samsung confirmed at its AGM that it will produce Tesla's AI6 chip on its 2nm process at the Taylor, Texas fab. $16.5B deal through 2033. But the prototype run is 6 months behind schedule due to yield issues.

Terafab breaks ground in 2 days (March 21). LG $4.3B battery deal already locked in. Both chip and battery supply chains are coming together.

Yet the stock won't budge from $398. P/E 395x, 43 analysts say Hold, target $408 (+3%). The market knows all of this already.

- AI6 prototype delayed 6 months (2nm yield issues)
- Terafab groundbreaking Mar 21 (D-2)
- LG $4.3B battery deal confirmed (Megapack 3)
- China Jan-Feb sales +35% YoY

Technicals
- 52wk high $498.83 / low $249.84
- Below 50-day MA ($415) / Above 200-day MA ($394)
- RSI 38 (neutral)

P/E: 395x / Forward P/E: 193x
Target: $408.42 (+3%, 43 analysts Hold)
Verdict: Hold (6-12 months)

$TSLA (Mar 19) $398.09. Samsung confirmed AI6 production. $16.5B deal. But the prototype is 6 months late. Tesla's hobby: announce big, deliver eventually.

empathy.hook: Tesla bulls, catalysts everywhere but the stock won't move
empathy.text:
$TSLA (Mar 19) $398.09. Samsung AI6 confirmed, Terafab D-2, LG battery deal. Great news keeps coming but the price just sits there. The wait is frustrating.

1/4
thread:
AI6 prototype being 6 months late is disappointing, but the $16.5B contract itself is locked in. Once Samsung's yields improve, 2027 production is real.

2/4

Terafab groundbreaking in 2 days, Cybercab production next month. The catalysts are about to become reality. China +35% is also a solid foundation.

3/4

Holding above 200-day MA, RSI 38 near oversold. Even with the P/E burden, long-term holders are in a patience game. This time will pass.

4/4

witty.hook: Tesla: building a chip fab, battery plant, and robotaxi factory simultaneously
witty.text:
$TSLA (Mar 19) $398.09

Samsung confirmed AI6 production but the prototype is 6 months late. $16.5B contract signed, yield not solved. Classic Tesla: sign the check first, figure it out later.

Terafab breaks ground in 2 days. Zero semiconductor experience, world's largest 2nm fab. LG battery $4.3B done, China +35%, Cybercab next month.

The catalyst list is impressive. The stock doesn't care. P/E 395x, 43 analysts say Hold, target $408. Patience.

empathy.hook: To Tesla holders wondering why $398 with all this good news
empathy.text:
$TSLA (Mar 19) $398.09

Samsung AI6 $16.5B confirmed. LG $4.3B battery done. Terafab in 2 days. China +35%. The news couldn't be better, yet the stock sits still. It's understandable to feel frustrated.

The AI6 delay is a concern, but the contract is locked. Terafab groundbreaking and Cybercab production are imminent. These catalysts are becoming real.

Holding above the 200-day MA with RSI near oversold. The P/E is steep but catalysts are materializing one by one. This patience could pay off.
